# Amazon SQS Connector - Full Documentation

# Amazon SQS Connector 5.12 Additional Configuration Information

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Configure additional functionality such as controlling polling behavior globally and locally,
receiving messages on the primary node only, creating the FIFO queue, and managing large payloads.

## Control Polling Behavior

Since version 5.9, the default behavior of the Receive Messages source and the Read operation is to
use long polling:

- With long polling, SQS Connector returns the query results as soon as possible, if any messages
  are present.

  Otherwise, SQS Connector waits the specified amount of seconds before returning an empty response.

- With short polling, SQS Connector always returns the query results as soon as possible, even if
  that means returning an empty response.

Long polling can reduce the number of network requests without affecting the throughput.

You can control polling behavior either globally or at the individual source and operation level.

### Control Polling Behavior Globally

To control polling behavior globally, use the Default Wait Time field on the Advanced tab of the
global element configuration screen for the Receive Messages source, Read operation, or both:

- Set this field to a value between `1` and `20` to use long polling. With long polling, if there
  are messages available immediately, the connector returns the query results immediately. If not,
  the connector waits up to the specified amount of seconds for a message to be added before
  returning an empty response.
- Set this field to `0` to use short polling. With short polling, the connector always returns the
  query results as soon as possible, even if that means returning an empty response.

In the following screenshot, the Default Wait Time field is set to `20`. This value directs the
connector to use long polling and to wait 20 seconds before returning an empty response.

![Default Wait Time Configuration](_images/amazon-sqs-config-wait-time.png)

### Control Polling Behavior Locally

To control polling behavior locally, use the Wait Time field on the Advanced tab of the Receive
Messages source properties window, the Read operation properties window, or both.

## Receive Messages on the Primary Node Only

The default behavior of the Receive Messages source is to receive messages on all nodes when running
in cluster mode.

If you want the app to receive messages on the primary node only:

1.  In Studio, select the **Receive Messages** source for your flow.
2.  On the **Advanced** sub-tab of the **Receive messages** tab, select the **Primary node only**
    checkbox:

    ![Read message configuration Advanced tab with Primary node only option selected](_images/amazon-sqs-primary-node-only.png)

The multi-node cluster behavior depends on the selected SQS queue type. When using the Standard
queue, SQS guarantees that a message is delivered at least once. However, duplicate messages might
be introduced in the queue. To avoid duplication of messages in the queue, you can use a FIFO
(First-In-First-Out) queue to ensure that a message is delivered only once and that it remains
available until a consumer processes and deletes it.

For more information about queue types, see
[AWS SQS Standard Queues](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/standard-queues.html)
and
[AWS SQS FIFO Queues](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/).

## Create the FIFO Queue

FIFO queues deliver messages in the order in which they are sent.

To create the FIFO queue, use the **Create queue** operation in the connector, and set the
**FifoQueue** and **ContentBasedDeduplication** field values to `true`.

End the **queue name** value with the `.fifo` extension, for example:  
`MyTestFIFOQueue.fifo`.

When you create the FIFO queues, select any of the 15 regions that Amazon supports.

When you use Amazon SQS Connector to send messages to the FIFO queue, use the same queue URL and
region name that you used when creating the FIFO queue. You must also provide a value for the
attribute `message group id` in the connector configuration while sending messages.

## Manage Large Payloads

To enable support for managing large message payloads in Amazon SQS Connector, follow these steps:

1.  In Studio, click the **Global Elements tab** at the base of the canvas.
2.  Select the **Amazon SQS Configuration** you previously created and click **Edit**.
3.  In the **Connection** section, click the **Advanced** tab.
4.  In the **Large Payload Support** field, choose either:
    - **Expression or Bean Reference**
    - **Edit Inline**

      ![Large Payload Support configuration](_images/amazon-sqs-studio-large-payload-support.png)

5.  Configure the parameters as follows:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Bucket</p></td><td><p>Name of the bucket to use for storing large message payloads. The bucket must already be created and configured in AWS S3. Enabling this feature incurs additional charges for using AWS S3.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Size Threshold</p></td><td><p>Message size threshold value for storing message payloads in the AWS S3 bucket. The default value for message size threshold is 256 KB and the maximum threshold size value is 256KB. The maximum message size is 2GB.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Size Threshold Unit</p></td><td><p>Represents the data unit for the message size threshold.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    After you specify the values for these fields, Amazon SQS Connector is ready to manage large
    payload messages and store them automatically in the specified AWS S3 bucket when the message
    size threshold value is exceeded.

For an example of sending and receiving messages with large payload messages, see
[Send and Receive Large Messages Example](amazon-sqs-connector-examples#send-and-receive-large-messages).

## Next

After you create the FIFO queue, you can try the
[Amazon SQS Connector Examples](amazon-sqs-connector-examples).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Amazon SQS Connector 5.12 Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The following examples show several Mule flows for Amazon SQS Connector:

- [Send and Receive Messages](#send-and-receive-messages)
- [Send and Receive Large Messages](#send-and-receive-large-messages)

## Send and Receive Messages

This example sends a message with metadata to an Amazon SQS queue and then receives the message from
the queue. The example contains two flows:

- The first flow sends a message with metadata and gets the count of the messages in the queue to
  validate that the message was sent.
- The second flow receives the message and logs the message body.

![Send and Receive Messages flows](_images/amazon-sqs-message-flow.png)

### Configure the First Flow

Configure the first flow that sends a message to the queue. Creating the first flow involves
configuring a **Listener** component, a **Transform Message** component, a **Send message**
operation, a **Logger** component, a **Get approximate number of messages** operation, and a second
**Logger** component.

#### Configure the HTTP Listener

Configure the **Listener** component to initiate a Mule flow when a call is made to the `/` path:

1.  Create a new Mule project in Studio.
2.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select **HTTP** and drag the **Listener** component to the
    canvas.
3.  In the properties window, click **+** next to the **Connector configuration** field to add a
    global element.
4.  Accept the defaults.
5.  In the properties window, set the **Path** field value to `/`.

#### Add the Transform Message Component

Add the **Transform Message** component to attach the metadata and convert the values from the HTTP
input form to Java format so they can be used as input to the **Send message** operation:

1.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select **Core** and drag a **Transform Message** component to
    the right of **Listener**.
2.  In the properties window, overlay the brackets in the **Output** section with this DataWeave
    code:

    ```dataweave
    %dw 2.0
    output application/java
    ---
    {
    	delaySeconds: 0,
    	body: "Hello World",
    	messageAttributes: {
    		"AccountId": {
    			"stringValue" : "000123456",
    			"dataType" : "String.AccountId"
    		} as Object {
    			class: "org.mule.extension.sqs.api.model.MessageAttributeValue"
    		},
    		"NumberId": {
    			"stringValue" : "230.000000000000000001",
    			"dataType" : "Number"
    		} as Object {
    			class : "org.mule.extension.sqs.api.model.MessageAttributeValue"
    		}
    	} as Object {
    		class: "java.util.HashMap"
    	}
    } as Object {
    	class: "org.mule.extension.sqs.api.model.Message"
    }
    ```

#### Add the Send Message Operation

Add the **Send message** operation to send the message to the specified queue:

1.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select **Amazon SQS** and drag the **Send message** operation to
    the right of **Transform Message**.
2.  In the properties window, click **+** next to the **Connector configuration** field to add a
    global element.
3.  Configure the global element as follows:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>Name used to reference the configuration.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Session Token</p></td><td><p>Session token used to validate the temporary security credentials.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access Key</p></td><td><p>Alphanumeric text string that uniquely identifies the user who owns the account.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Secret Key</p></td><td><p>Key that acts as a password.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Region Endpoint</p></td><td><p>Queue region.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SQS Endpoint</p></td><td><p>SQS endpoint for Amazon VPC support.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Default Global Queue URL</p></td><td><p>Default Amazon SQS queue URL credentials.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    The following image shows an example of the **Send Message** configuration of the global
    element:

    ![Send Message global element configuration window](_images/amazon-sqs-studio-global-config-new.png)

4.  In the properties window, configure the following fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Name for the connector operation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector configuration</p></td><td><p>Global configuration you just created.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message</p></td><td><p><code>payload</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Queue url</p></td><td><p>Amazon SQS queue URL.</p><p>If provided, the value of this field takes precedence over the value of the <strong>Default Global Queue URL</strong> field on the <strong>Global Configuration Elements</strong> window.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    The following image shows an example of the **Send Message** configuration in the properties
    window:

    ![Send Message properties window configuration](_images/amazon-sqs-send-message.png)

#### Add the First Logger Component

Add the first **Logger** component to display the response in the Mule console:

1.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select **Core** and drag a **Logger** component to the right of
    **Send message**.
2.  In the properties window, configure the following fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Name for the logger, such as <code>Log Response</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message</p></td><td><p><code>Sent Message: `#[payload]`</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Level</p></td><td><p>INFO (Default)</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    The following image shows an example of the **Logger** configuration in the properties window:

    ![Logger properties window configuration](_images/amazon-sqs-logger.png)

#### Add the Get Approximate Number of Messages Operation

Add the **Get approximate number of messages** operation to obtain the number of messages in the
queue:

1.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select **Amazon SQS** and drag the **Get approximate number of
    messages** operation to the right of **Logger**.
2.  In the properties window, configure the following fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Name for the connector operation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector configuration</p></td><td><p>Global configuration you just created.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Queue url</p></td><td><p><code>${sqs.queueUrl}</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    The following image shows an example of the **Get approximate number of messages** configuration
    in the properties window:

    ![Get Approximate Number of Messages properties window configuration](_images/amazon-sqs-get-message-count.png)

#### Add the Second Logger Component

Add the second **Logger** component to display the message count in the Mule console:

1.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select **Core** and drag a **Logger** component to the right of
    **Get approximate number of messages**.
2.  In the properties window, configure the following fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Name for the logger, such as <code>Log Count</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message</p></td><td><p><code>Sent Message: `#[payload]`</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Level</p></td><td><p>INFO (Default)</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    The following image shows an example of the **Logger** configuration in the properties window:

    ![Logger properties window configuration](_images/amazon-sqs-logger2.png)

### Configure the Second Flow

Configure the second flow that receives and logs the messages before the messages are deleted from
the queue. Creating the second flow involves configuring a **Receive messages** source and a
**Logger** component.

#### Add the Receive Messages Source

Add the **Receive messages** source to retrieve messages:

1.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select **Amazon SQS** and drag the **Receive messages** source
    to the canvas.
2.  In the properties window, configure the following fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Name for the connector operation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector configuration</p></td><td><p>Global configuration you just created.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Number of messages</p></td><td><p><code>1</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Queue url</p></td><td><p>Amazon SQS queue URL.</p><p>If provided, the value of this field takes precedence over the value of the <strong>Default Global Queue URL</strong> field on the <strong>Global Configuration Elements</strong> window.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    The following image shows an example of the **Receive messages** configuration in the properties
    window:

    ![Receive Messages properties window configuration](_images/amazon-sqs-receive-message.png)

#### Add the Logger Component

Add the **Logger** component to display the message in the Mule console:

1.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select **Core** and drag a **Logger** component to the right of
    **Receive messages**.
2.  In the properties window, configure the following fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Name for the logger, such as <code>Log Receipt</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message</p></td><td><p><code>`#[payload]`</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Level</p></td><td><p>INFO (Default)</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    The following image shows an example of the **Logger** configuration in the properties window:

    ![Logger properties window configuration](_images/amazon-sqs-logger-receive.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<mule xmlns:sqs="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sqs" xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
  http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
  http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
  http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
  http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sqs
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sqs/current/mule-sqs.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<sqs:config name="Amazon_SQS_Configuration" doc:name="Amazon SQS Configuration"
	defaultQueueUrl="${sqs.queueUrl}" >
		<sqs:basic-connection accessKey="${sqs.accessKey}" secretKey="$sqs.secretKey}" region="us-east-1"/>
	</sqs:config>
	<flow name="sqs-send-messageFlow" >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener"
		config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config"
		path="/"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" >
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/java
---
{
	delaySeconds: 0,
	body: "Hello World",
	messageAttributes: {
		"AccountId": {
			"stringValue" : "000123456",
			"dataType" : "String.AccountId"
		} as Object {
			class: "org.mule.extension.sqs.api.model.MessageAttributeValue"
		},
		"NumberId": {
			"stringValue" : "230.000000000000000001",
			"dataType" : "Number"
		} as Object {
			class : "org.mule.extension.sqs.api.model.MessageAttributeValue"
		}
	} as Object {
		class: "java.util.HashMap"
	}
} as Object {
	class: "org.mule.extension.sqs.api.model.Message"
}]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
		<sqs:send-message doc:name="Send message"/>
		<logger level="INFO"
		doc:name="Log Response"
		message="payload"/>
		<sqs:get-approximate-number-of-messages
		doc:name="Get approximate number of messages"
		config-ref="Amazon_SQS_Configuration"
		queueUrl="${sqs.queueUrl}"/>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Log Count"
		message="Sent Message: `#[payload]`"/>
	</flow>
	<flow name="sqs-receive-message-flow" >
		<sqs:receivemessages doc:name="Receive messages"
		config-ref="Amazon_SQS_Configuration"/>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Log Receipt" />
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## Send and Receive Large Messages

> [!NOTE] Enabling this feature incurs additional charges associated with using AWS S3.

This example sends a large message with metadata to an Amazon SQS queue that references the AWS S3
bucket to use for storing large message payloads, and then receives the large message from the
queue. The example contains two flows:

- The first flow sends a large message with metadata and gets the count of the messages in the queue
  to validate that the large message was sent.
- The second flow receives the large message and logs the message body.

![Send and Receive Large Messages flows](_images/amazon-sqs-large-message-flow.png)

### Before You Begin

Before you begin, you must have:

- Access to the Amazon SQS target resource and Anypoint Platform
- A file containing a payload larger than 256 KB
- An AWS S3 bucket created on AWS S3 to use for storing the large payload messages
- AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) credentials

### Configure the First Flow

Configure the first flow that sends a large message to the queue. Creating the first flow involves
configuring a **Listener** component, a **Read** operation, a **Transform Message** component, a
**Send Message** operation, a **Logger** component, a **Get approximate number of messages**
operation, and a second **Logger** component.

#### Configure the HTTP Listener

Configure the **Listener** component to initiate a Mule flow when a call is made to the
`/largePayload` path:

1.  Create a new Mule project in Studio.
2.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select **HTTP** and drag the **Listener** component to the
    canvas.
3.  In the properties window, click **+** next to the **Connector configuration** field to add a
    global element.
4.  Accept the defaults.
5.  In the properties window, set the **Path** field value to `/largePayload`.

#### Add the Read Operation

Add the **Read** operation to read the large payload:

1.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select **File** and drag the **Read** operation to the right of
    **Listener**.
2.  In the properties window, click **+** next to the **Connector configuration** field to add a
    global element.
3.  Select the **Connection** checkbox.
4.  In **Working Directory**, enter the directory that will serve as the root directory for every
    relative path used with this connector.  
    The default is your user home directory.

    In this example, the file is read from `src/main/resources` of the Mule application:

    ![Working Directory configuration window for Read operation](_images/amazon-sqs-file-global-config.png)

5.  In the properties window, configure the following fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Name for the connector operation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector configuration</p></td><td><p>Global configuration you just created.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Path</p></td><td><p>Path to the file to read.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    The following image shows an example of the **Read** configuration in the properties window:

    ![Read properties window configuration](_images/amazon-sqs-file-config.png)

#### Add the Transform Message Component

Add the **Transform Message** component to attach the metadata and convert the values from the HTTP
input form to Java format so they can be used as input to the **Send message** operation:

1.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select **Core** and drag a **Transform Message** component to
    the right of **Read**.
2.  In the properties window, overlay the brackets in the **Output** section with this DataWeave
    code:

    ```dataweave
    %dw 2.0
    output application/java
    ---
    {
    	delaySeconds: 0,
    	body: "Hello World",
    	messageAttributes: {
    		"AccountId": {
    			"stringValue" : "000123456",
    			"dataType" : "String.AccountId"
    		} as Object {
    			class: "org.mule.extension.sqs.api.model.MessageAttributeValue"
    		},
    		"NumberId": {
    			"stringValue" : "230.000000000000000001",
    			"dataType" : "Number"
    		} as Object {
    			class : "org.mule.extension.sqs.api.model.MessageAttributeValue"
    		}
    	} as Object {
    		class: "java.util.HashMap"
    	}
    } as Object {
    	class: "org.mule.extension.sqs.api.model.Message"
    }
    ```

#### Add the Send Message Operation

Add the **Send message** operation to send the large message to the specified queue:

1.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select **Amazon SQS** and drag the **Send message** operation to
    the right of **Transform Message**.
2.  In the properties window, click **+** next to the **Connector configuration** field to add a
    global element.
3.  Configure the global element as follows:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>Name used to reference the configuration.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Session Token</p></td><td><p>Session token used to validate the temporary security credentials.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access Key</p></td><td><p>Alphanumeric text string that uniquely identifies the user who owns the account.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Secret Key</p></td><td><p>Key that acts as a password.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Region Endpoint</p></td><td><p>Queue region.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SQS Endpoint</p></td><td><p>SQS endpoint for Amazon VPC support.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Default Global Queue URL</p></td><td><p>Default Amazon SQS queue URL credentials.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    The following image shows an example of the **Send Message** configuration of the global
    element:

    ![Send Message global element configuration window](_images/amazon-sqs-studio-global-config-new.png)

4.  In the **Connection** section, click the **Advanced** tab.
5.  In the **Large Payload Support** field, select one of the following:
    - **Expression or Bean reference**
    - **Edit inline**

      Configure the fields as follows:

      <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Bucket</p></td><td><p>Name of the AWS S3 bucket to use for storing large message payloads. The bucket must already be created and configured in AWS S3. Enabling this feature incurs additional charges for using AWS S3.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Size Threshold</p></td><td><p>Message size threshold for storing message payloads in the AWS S3 bucket. The default value for message size threshold is 256 KB and the maximum threshold size value is 256 KB. The maximum message size is 2 GB.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Size Threshold Unit</p></td><td><p>Data unit for the message size threshold.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom S3 Endpoint</p></td><td><p>Amazon S3 endpoint URL for VPC support.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

      The following image shows an example of the **Large Payload Support** fields configuration:

      ![Large Payload Support fields configuration window](_images/amazon-sqs-studio-large-payload-support.png)

6.  In the properties window, configure the following fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Name for the connector operation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector configuration</p></td><td><p>Global configuration you just created.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message</p></td><td><p><code>payload</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Queue url</p></td><td><p>Amazon SQS queue URL.</p><p>If provided, the value of this field takes precedence over the value of the <strong>Default Global Queue URL</strong> field on the <strong>Global Configuration Elements</strong> window.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    The following image shows an example of the **Send Message** configuration in the properties
    window:

    ![Send Message properties window configuration](_images/amazon-sqs-send-message.png)

#### Add the First Logger Component

Add the first **Logger** component to display the response in the Mule console:

1.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select **Core** and drag a **Logger** component to the right of
    **Send message**.
2.  In the properties window, configure the following fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Name for the logger, such as <code>Log Response</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message</p></td><td><p><code>Sent Message: `#[payload]`</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Level</p></td><td><p>INFO (Default)</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    The following image shows an example of the **Logger** configuration in the properties window:

    ![Logger properties window configuration](_images/amazon-sqs-logger.png)

#### Add the Get Approximate Number of Messages Operation

Add the **Get approximate number of messages** operation to obtain the number of messages in the
queue:

1.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select **Amazon SQS** and drag the **Get approximate number of
    messages** operation to the right of **Logger**.
2.  In the properties window, configure the following fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Name for the connector operation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector configuration</p></td><td><p>Global configuration you just created.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Queue url</p></td><td><p><code>${sqs.queueUrl}</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    The following image shows an example of the **Get approximate number of messages** configuration
    in the properties window:

    ![Get Approximate Number of Messages properties window configuration](_images/amazon-sqs-get-message-count.png)

#### Add the Second Logger Component

Add the second **Logger** component to display the message count in the Mule console:

1.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select **Core** and drag a **Logger** component to the right of
    **Get approximate number of messages**.
2.  In the properties window, configure the following fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Name for the logger, such as <code>Log Count</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message</p></td><td><p><code>Sent Message: `#[payload]`</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Level</p></td><td><p>INFO (Default)</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    The following image shows an example of the **Logger** configuration in the properties window:

    ![Logger properties window configuration](_images/amazon-sqs-logger2.png)

### Configure the Second Flow

Configure the second flow that receives and logs the large messages before the large messages are
deleted from the queue. Creating the second flow involves configuring a **Receive messages** source
and a **Logger** component.

#### Add the Receive Messages Source

Add the **Receive messages** source to retrieve large messages:

1.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select **Amazon SQS** and drag the **Receive messages** source
    to the canvas.
2.  In the properties window, configure the following fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Name for the connector operation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector configuration</p></td><td><p>Global configuration you just created.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Number of messages</p></td><td><p><code>1</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Queue url</p></td><td><p>Amazon SQS queue URL.</p><p>If provided, the value of this field takes precedence over the value of the <strong>Default Global Queue URL</strong> field on the <strong>Global Configuration Elements</strong> window.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    The following image shows an example of the **Receive messages** configuration in the properties
    window:

    ![Receive Messages properties window configuration](_images/amazon-sqs-receive-message.png)

#### Add the Logger Component

Add the **Logger** component to display the large message in the Mule console:

1.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select **Core** and drag a **Logger** component to the right of
    **Receive messages**.
2.  In the properties window, configure the following fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Name for the logger, such as <code>Log Receipt</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message</p></td><td><p><code>`#[payload]`</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Level</p></td><td><p>INFO (Default)</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    The following image shows an example of the **Logger** configuration in the properties window:

    ![Logger properties window configuration](_images/amazon-sqs-logger-receive.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<mule xmlns:sqs="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sqs" xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
  http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
  http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
  http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
  http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file
  http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
  http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sqs
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sqs/current/mule-sqs.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>

	<sqs:config name="Amazon_SQS_Configuration" doc:name="Amazon SQS Configuration"
	defaultQueueUrl="${sqs.queueUrl}" >
		<sqs:basic-connection accessKey="${sqs.accessKey}" secretKey="$sqs.secretKey}" region="us-east-1" />
	</sqs:config>

	<sqs:config name="Amazon_SQS_Large_Payload_Configuration" doc:name="Amazon SQS Configuration"
	defaultQueueUrl="${sqs.queueUrl}" >
		<sqs:basic-connection accessKey="${sqs.accessKey}" secretKey="$sqs.secretKey}" region="us-east-1" >
			<sqs:large-payload-support bucket="large-sqs-payload-bucket" messageSizeThreshold="256" messageSizeThresholdUnit="KB" />
		</sqs:basic-connection>
	</sqs:config>

	<file:config name="File_Config" doc:name="File Config" >
		<file:connection workingDir="${app.home}" />
	</file:config>

	<flow name="sqs-send-LargeMessageFlow" >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener"
		config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config"
		path="/largePayload"/>
		<file:read doc:name="Read" config-ref="File_Config" path="largePayload.txt"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" >
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/java
---
{
	delaySeconds: 0,
	body: payload,
	messageAttributes: {
		"AccountId": {
			"stringValue" : "000123456",
			"dataType" : "String.AccountId"
		} as Object {
			class: "org.mule.extension.sqs.api.model.MessageAttributeValue"
		},
		"NumberId": {
			"stringValue" : "230.000000000000000001",
			"dataType" : "Number"
		} as Object {
			class : "org.mule.extension.sqs.api.model.MessageAttributeValue"
		}
	} as Object {
		class: "java.util.HashMap"
	}
} as Object {
	class: "org.mule.extension.sqs.api.model.Message"
}]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
		<sqs:send-message doc:name="Send message" configref="Amazon_SQS_Large_Payload_Configuration" config-ref="Amazon_SQS_Large_Payload_Configuration"/>
		<logger level="INFO"
		doc:name="Log Response"
		message="payload"/>
		<sqs:get-approximate-number-of-messages
		doc:name="Get approximate number of messages"
		config-ref="Amazon_SQS_Large_Payload_Configuration"
		queueUrl="${sqs.queueUrl}"/>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Log Count"
		message="Sent Message: `#[payload]`"/>
	</flow>
	<flow name="sqs-receive-large-message-flow" >
		<sqs:receivemessages doc:name="Receive messages"
		config-ref="Amazon_SQS_Large_Payload_Configuration"/>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Log Receipt" />
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Amazon SQS Connector 5.12 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

> [!NOTE] Where possible, we changed noninclusive terms to align with our company value of Equality.
> We maintained certain terms to avoid any effect on customer implementations.

Anypoint Connector for Amazon SQS (Amazon SQS Connector) enables you to interface with the Amazon
Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) API and manage SQS queueing services without having to interact
with the Amazon SQS API directly.

## Configuration

Default Configuration for Amazon SQS Connector.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#config_basic">Basic</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#config_role">Role</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Connection types for this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Default Global Queue URL</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This value is used when you do not set the queue URL at the operation level. If you do not specify the queue URL here or at the operation level, an exception is thrown.</p></div><div><p>A queue URL is constructed in the following format:&nbsp;<code>https://sqs.{REGION}.amazonaws.com/{ACCOUNT_NUMBER}/{QUEUE_NAME}</code>, for example: <code>https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789101/test-queue</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Default Wait Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Default value for the maximum duration of a single poll, in seconds:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Set this field to a value between <code>1</code> and <code>20</code> to use long polling. With long polling, if there are messages available immediately, the connector returns the query results immediately. If not, the connector waits up to the specified amount of seconds for a message to be added before returning an empty response.</p></li><li><p>Set this field to <code>0</code> to use short polling. With short polling, the connector always returns the query results as soon as possible, even if that means returning an empty response.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>20</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expiration Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Basic Connection Type

Uses a session token provided by Amazon STS to connect to Amazon SQS.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Session Token</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Session token provided by Amazon STS.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Test Queue ARN</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Use this parameter to test the connection to Amazon SQS Connector. If you do not specify this parameter, the test connection is not performed.</p></div><div><p>A queue ARN is constructed in the following format: <code>arn:aws:{REGION}:{ACCOUNT_NUMBER}:{QUEUE_NAME}</code>, for example:&nbsp;<code>arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789101:test-queue</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Large Payload Support</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#LargePayloadSupport">Large Payload Support</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Support large message payloads on Amazon SQS.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service. A value of <code>0</code> means infinity and is not recommended.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>50000</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>MILLISECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Connections</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the maximum number of allowed open HTTP connections.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Socket Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long to wait for data to be transferred over an established, open connection before the connection times out. A value of <code>0</code> means infinity and is not recommended.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>50000</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Socket Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Socket Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>MILLISECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Access Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Access key provided by Amazon.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Secret Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Secret key provided by Amazon.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Try Default AWSCredentials Provider Chain</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set this field to <code>true</code> to obtain credentials from the AWS environment. Refer to <a href="https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdk-for-java/latest/developer-guide/credentials.html">Using Credentials</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Region Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the topic region endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>US_EAST_1</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom SQS Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets a custom SQS endpoint. Use this parameter when a non-standard service endpoint is required, such as a Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional proxy host.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional proxy port.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional proxy username.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional proxy password.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Domain</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional proxy domain.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Workstation</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional proxy workstation.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Role Connection Type

Configures the Amazon role ARN that uniquely identifies the role to assume to allow cross-account
access.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Role ARN</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Role ARN that uniquely identifies the role to assume to gain cross-account access.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>External ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A unique identifier that might be required when you assume a role in another account. If the administrator of the account to which the role belongs provides an external ID, then provide that value in this field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Test Queue ARN</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Use this parameter to test the connection to Amazon SQS Connector. If you do not specify this parameter, the test connection is not performed.</p></div><div><p>A queue ARN is constructed in the following format: <code>arn:aws:{REGION}:{ACCOUNT_NUMBER}:{QUEUE_NAME}</code>, for example:&nbsp;<code>arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789101:test-queue</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Large Payload Support</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#LargePayloadSupport">Large Payload Support</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Support large message payloads on Amazon SQS.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service. A value of <code>0</code> means infinity and is not recommended.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>50000</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>MILLISECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Connections</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the maximum number of allowed open HTTP connections.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Socket Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long to wait for data to be transferred over an established, open connection before the connection times out. A value of <code>0</code> means infinity and is not recommended.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>50000</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Socket Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Socket Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>MILLISECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Access Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Access key provided by Amazon.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Secret Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Secret key provided by Amazon.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Try Default AWSCredentials Provider Chain</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set this field to <code>true</code> to obtain credentials from the AWS environment. Refer to <a href="https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdk-for-java/latest/developer-guide/credentials.html">Using Credentials</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Region Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the topic region endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>US_EAST_1</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom STS Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets a custom STS endpoint. Use this parameter when a non-standard service endpoint is required, such as a Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) endpoint. The custom STS endpoint might not work if <code>DefaultAWSCredentialsProviderChain</code> is used. Instead, use AWS environment variables such as <code>AWS_STS_REGIONAL_ENDPOINTS</code>, <code>AWS_REGION</code>, and <code>AWS_ENDPOINT_URL</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom SQS Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets a custom SQS endpoint. Use this parameter when a non-standard service endpoint is required, such as a Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional proxy host.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional proxy port.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional proxy username.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional proxy password.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Domain</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional proxy domain.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Workstation</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Optional proxy workstation.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Sources

- [Receive messages](#receivemessages)

### Receive messages

`<sqs:receivemessages>`

Retrieves one or more messages from a server but triggers a flow for each message separately.

> [!NOTE] In Amazon SQS Connector 5.12.5 and later, the **Receive messages** source performs initial
> validation of the queue URL. If the validation fails, the source stops running. To enable queue
> URL validation, ensure that the IAM role includes an additional policy granting the
> `sqs:GetQueueAttributes` permission.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Visibility Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Period of time during which other consumers are prevented from receiving and processing the same message in the queue.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>30</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Visibility Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Visibility Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Preserve Messages</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Preserve messages after they are read rather than deleting them immediately from the queue after they are read.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Number Of Messages</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of messages to retrieve on each call. The maximum is <code>10</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>1</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the queue in which to receive messages.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Wait time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum duration of a single poll, in seconds:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Set this field to a value between <code>1</code> and <code>20</code> to use long polling. With long polling, if there are messages available immediately, the connector returns the query results immediately. If not, the connector waits up to the specified amount of seconds for a message to be added before returning an empty response.</p></li><li><p>Set this field to <code>0</code> to use short polling. With short polling, the connector always returns the query results as soon as possible, even if that means returning an empty response.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Number of consuming threads</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of threads used to consume the messages in the inbound flow. This value must be an integer greater than <code>0</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>1</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Primary Node Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running Mule instances in a cluster.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Frequency at which the connector polls for incoming messages, in the time unit specified in the <strong>Time unit</strong> field. If you set this value to <code>0</code>, the connector polls as fast as possible.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>1000</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Start delay</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount of time that the connector waits before it starts polling for incoming messages, in the time unit specified in the <strong>Time unit</strong> field. The default value of <code>0</code> means that the connector starts polling immediately.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>0</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Frequency</strong> and <strong>Start delay</strong> fields.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>MILLISECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Redelivery Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

## Operations

- [Delete Message Batch](#deleteMessageBatch)
- [Delete Queue](#deleteQueue)
- [Get Approximate Number Of Messages](#getApproximateNumberOfMessages)
- [Get Queue Attributes](#getQueueAttributes)
- [Get Queue Url](#getQueueUrl)
- [List Dead Letter Source Queues](#listDeadLetterSourceQueues)
- [List Queues](#listQueues)
- [Purge Queue](#purgeQueue)
- [Read](#read)
- [Remove Permission](#removePermission)
- [Send Message](#sendMessage)
- [Send Message Batch](#sendMessageBatch)
- [Set Queue Attributes](#setQueueAttributes)

### Add Permission

`<sqs:add-permission>`

Adds a permission to a message queue.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Label</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name for this permission.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Account Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>IDs of the AWS accounts to share this queue with.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Actions</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List to indicate how much to share (<strong>Send Message</strong>, <strong>Receive Message</strong>, <strong>Change Message Visibility</strong>, <strong>Delete Message</strong>, and <strong>Get Queue Attributes</strong>).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the queue in which to add permissions to. This parameter is optional and if unspecified, you must specify the Default Global Queue URL at the configuration level.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SQS:ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:CONNECTIVITY
- SQS:INTERNAL_FAILURE
- SQS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SQS:INVALID_DATA
- SQS:OPT_IN_REQUIRED
- SQS:OVER_LIMIT
- SQS:REQUEST_EXPIRED
- SQS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SQS:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SQS:THROTTLING_EXCEPTION

### Change Message Visibility

`<sqs:change-message-visibility>`

Changes the visibility timeout of a specified message in a queue to a new value, not to exceed 12
hours.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Receipt Handle</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Receipt handle associated with the message whose visibility timeout must change.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Visibility Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>New value of the message visibility timeout (up to 4300 seconds or 12 hours).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Visibility Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Visibility Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the queue to act on. This parameter is optional and if unspecified, you must specify the Default Global Queue URL at the configuration level.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SQS:ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:CONNECTIVITY
- SQS:INTERNAL_FAILURE
- SQS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SQS:INVALID_DATA
- SQS:MESSAGE_NOT_IN_FLIGHT
- SQS:MESSAGE_SIZE_THRESHOLD_OUT_OF_RANGE
- SQS:OPT_IN_REQUIRED
- SQS:PARAMETER_OUT_OF_RANGE
- SQS:REQUEST_EXPIRED
- SQS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SQS:S3_BUCKET_ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:S3_BUCKET_NOT_FOUND
- SQS:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SQS:THROTTLING_EXCEPTION

### Change Message Visibility Batch

`<sqs:change-message-visibility-batch>`

Changes the visibility timeout of up to 10 ChangeMessageVisibility requests, with each result
reported individually in the response.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Receipt Handles</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ChangeMessageVisibilityBatchRequestEntry">Change Message Visibility Batch Request Entry</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of receipt handles of the messages for which the visibility timeout must be changed.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the queue to act on. This parameter is optional and if unspecified, you must specify the Default Global Queue URL at the configuration level.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#BatchResult">Batch Result</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SQS:ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:CONNECTIVITY
- SQS:INTERNAL_FAILURE
- SQS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SQS:INVALID_DATA
- SQS:MESSAGE_SIZE_THRESHOLD_OUT_OF_RANGE
- SQS:OPT_IN_REQUIRED
- SQS:PARAMETER_OUT_OF_RANGE
- SQS:REQUEST_EXPIRED
- SQS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SQS:S3_BUCKET_ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:S3_BUCKET_NOT_FOUND
- SQS:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SQS:THROTTLING_EXCEPTION

### Create Queue

`<sqs:create-queue>`

Creates a new queue or returns the URL of an existing one.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the queue to create.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map of attributes with their corresponding values. Refer to the <a href="#queue-attributes">Queue Attributes</a> table below.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Queue Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>DelaySeconds</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Length of time, in seconds, for which the delivery of all messages in the queue is delayed. Valid values are an integer from <code>0</code> to <code>900</code> seconds (15 minutes).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>0</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>MaximumMessageSize</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Limit of how many bytes a message can contain before Amazon SQS rejects it. Valid values are an integer from <code>1024</code> bytes (1 KiB) to <code>262,144</code> bytes (256 KiB).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>262,144</code> (256 KiB)</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>MessageRetentionPeriod</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Length of time, in seconds, for which Amazon SQS retains a message. Valid values are an integer from <code>60</code> seconds (1 minute) to <code>1,209,600</code> seconds (14 days).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>345,600</code> (4 days)</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Queueâs policy. A valid AWS policy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>ReceiveMessageWaitTimeSeconds</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Length of time, in seconds, for which a ReceiveMessage action waits for a message to arrive. Valid values are an integer from <code>0</code> to <code>20</code> (seconds).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>0</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>RedrivePolicy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String that includes the parameters for the dead-letter queue functionality of the source queue as a JSON object.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>VisibilityTimeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Visibility timeout for the queue, in seconds. Valid values are an integer from <code>0</code> to <code>43,200</code> (12 hours).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>30</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>KmsDataKeyReusePeriodSeconds</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Length of time, in seconds, for which Amazon SQS can reuse a data key to encrypt or decrypt messages before calling AWS KMS again. Valid values are an integer representing seconds, between <code>60</code> seconds (1 minute) and <code>86,400</code> seconds (24 hours).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>300</code> (5 minutes)</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>FifoQueue</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Designates a queue as FIFO. Valid values are <code>true</code> or <code>false</code>. If you donât specify this attribute, Amazon SQS creates a standard queue. You must provide this attribute during queue creation, and you canât change it for an existing queue. When you set this attribute, you must also explicitly provide the MessageGroupId for your messages.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>ContentBasedDeduplication</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables content-based deduplication. Valid values are <code>true</code> or <code>false</code>. Every message must have a unique MessageDeduplicationId.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>ApproximateNumberOfMessages</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Approximate number of messages available for retrieval from the queue.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>ApproximateNumberOfMessagesDelayed</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Approximate number of messages in the queue that are delayed and not available for reading immediately. This can happen when the queue is configured as a delay queue or when a message has been sent with a delay parameter.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>ApproximateNumberOfMessagesNotVisible</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Approximate number of messages that are in flight. Messages are considered to be in flight if they have been sent to a client but have not yet been deleted or have not yet reached the end of their visibility window.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>CreatedTimestamp</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time when the queue was created, in seconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>LastModifiedTimestamp</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time when the queue was last changed, in seconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>QueueArn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amazon resource name (ARN) of the queue.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Redrive Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>deadLetterTargetArn</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the dead-letter queue to which Amazon SQS moves messages after the value of maxReceiveCount is exceeded.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>maxReceiveCount</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of times a message is delivered to the source queue before being moved to the dead-letter queue. When the ReceiveCount for a message exceeds the maxReceiveCount for a queue, Amazon SQS moves the message to the dead-letter queue.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SQS:ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:CONNECTIVITY
- SQS:INTERNAL_FAILURE
- SQS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SQS:INVALID_DATA
- SQS:OPT_IN_REQUIRED
- SQS:QUEUE_ALREADY_EXISTS
- SQS:QUEUE_DELETED_RECENTLY
- SQS:REQUEST_EXPIRED
- SQS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SQS:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SQS:THROTTLING_EXCEPTION

### Delete Message

`<sqs:delete-message>`

Deletes the message identified by the message object in the queue.
