<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Receipt Handle</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Receipt handle of the message to delete.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the queue. This parameter is optional and if unspecified, you must specify the Default Global Queue URL at the configuration level.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SQS:ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:CONNECTIVITY
- SQS:INTERNAL_FAILURE
- SQS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SQS:INVALID_DATA
- SQS:MESSAGE_SIZE_THRESHOLD_OUT_OF_RANGE
- SQS:OPT_IN_REQUIRED
- SQS:PARAMETER_OUT_OF_RANGE
- SQS:REQUEST_EXPIRED
- SQS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SQS:S3_BUCKET_ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:S3_BUCKET_NOT_FOUND
- SQS:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SQS:THROTTLING_EXCEPTION

### Delete Message Batch

`<sqs:delete-message-batch>`

Deletes up to 10 messages from the specified queue. This is a batch version of **Delete Message**.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Entries</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DeleteMessageBatchRequestEntry">Delete Message Batch Request Entry</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of receipt handles for the messages to delete.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the queue to delete messages as a batch from. This parameter is optional and if unspecified, you must specify the Default Global Queue URL at the configuration level.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#BatchResult">Batch Result</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SQS:ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:CONNECTIVITY
- SQS:INTERNAL_FAILURE
- SQS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SQS:INVALID_DATA
- SQS:MESSAGE_SIZE_THRESHOLD_OUT_OF_RANGE
- SQS:OPT_IN_REQUIRED
- SQS:PARAMETER_OUT_OF_RANGE
- SQS:REQUEST_EXPIRED
- SQS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SQS:S3_BUCKET_ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:S3_BUCKET_NOT_FOUND
- SQS:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SQS:THROTTLING_EXCEPTION

### Delete Queue

`<sqs:delete-queue>`

Deletes the message queue represented by this object and can even delete a non-empty queue. Because
deleting a queue can take up to 60 seconds, wait at least that long before you create a new queue
with the same name.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the queue to delete. This parameter is optional and if unspecified, you must specify the Default Global Queue URL at the configuration level.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SQS:ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:CONNECTIVITY
- SQS:INTERNAL_FAILURE
- SQS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SQS:INVALID_DATA
- SQS:OPT_IN_REQUIRED
- SQS:REQUEST_EXPIRED
- SQS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SQS:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SQS:THROTTLING_EXCEPTION

### Get Approximate Number Of Messages

`<sqs:get-approximate-number-of-messages>`

Retrieves an approximate number of visible messages for a queue.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the queue.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SQS:ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:CONNECTIVITY
- SQS:INTERNAL_FAILURE
- SQS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SQS:INVALID_DATA
- SQS:OPT_IN_REQUIRED
- SQS:REQUEST_EXPIRED
- SQS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SQS:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SQS:THROTTLING_EXCEPTION

### Get Queue Attributes

`<sqs:get-queue-attributes>`

Shows queue attributes to expose the underlying functionality.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attribute Names</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of attributes to retrieve information for.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the queue to take action on. This parameter is optional and if unspecified, you must specify the Default Global Queue URL at the configuration level.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SQS:ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:CONNECTIVITY
- SQS:INTERNAL_FAILURE
- SQS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SQS:INVALID_DATA
- SQS:OPT_IN_REQUIRED
- SQS:REQUEST_EXPIRED
- SQS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SQS:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SQS:THROTTLING_EXCEPTION

### Get Queue Url

`<sqs:get-queue-url>`

Returns the URL of an existing queue.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the queue whose URL must be fetched.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Owner AWS Account Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AWS account ID of the owner that created the queue.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SQS:ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:CONNECTIVITY
- SQS:INTERNAL_FAILURE
- SQS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SQS:INVALID_DATA
- SQS:OPT_IN_REQUIRED
- SQS:REQUEST_EXPIRED
- SQS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SQS:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SQS:THROTTLING_EXCEPTION

### List Dead Letter Source Queues

`<sqs:list-dead-letter-source-queues>`

Returns a list of the queues that have the Redrive Policy queue attribute configured with a
dead-letter queue.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of a dead-letter queue. This parameter is optional and if unspecified, you must specify the Default Global Queue URL at the configuration level.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SQS:ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:CONNECTIVITY
- SQS:INTERNAL_FAILURE
- SQS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SQS:INVALID_DATA
- SQS:OPT_IN_REQUIRED
- SQS:REQUEST_EXPIRED
- SQS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SQS:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SQS:THROTTLING_EXCEPTION

### List Queues

`<sqs:list-queues>`

Returns a list of your queues. The maximum number of queues that can be returned is 1000.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Name Prefix</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String to use for filtering the list results. Queues only with names beginning with the specified string are returned.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SQS:ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:CONNECTIVITY
- SQS:INTERNAL_FAILURE
- SQS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SQS:INVALID_DATA
- SQS:OPT_IN_REQUIRED
- SQS:REQUEST_EXPIRED
- SQS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SQS:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SQS:THROTTLING_EXCEPTION

### Purge Queue

`<sqs:purge-queue>`

Deletes the messages in a queue specified by the queue URL.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the queue from which messages are fetched. This parameter is optional and if unspecified, you must specify the Default Global Queue URL at the configuration level.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SQS:ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:CONNECTIVITY
- SQS:INTERNAL_FAILURE
- SQS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SQS:INVALID_DATA
- SQS:OPT_IN_REQUIRED
- SQS:PURGE_QUEUE_IN_PROGRESS
- SQS:REQUEST_EXPIRED
- SQS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SQS:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SQS:THROTTLING_EXCEPTION

### Read

`<sqs:read>`

Reads a number of messages from a queue.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the queue.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Number Of Messages</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of messages to read.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Wait time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum duration of a single poll, in seconds:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Set this field to a value between <code>1</code> and <code>20</code> to use long polling.</p><div><p>With long polling, if there are messages available immediately, the connector returns the query results immediately. If not, the connector waits up to the specified amount of seconds for a message to be added before returning an empty response.</p></div></li><li><p>Set this field to <code>0</code> to use short polling. With short polling, the connector always returns the query results as soon as possible, even if that means returning an empty response.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of <a href="#Message">Message</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SQS:ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:CONNECTIVITY
- SQS:INTERNAL_FAILURE
- SQS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SQS:INVALID_DATA
- SQS:MESSAGE_SIZE_THRESHOLD_OUT_OF_RANGE
- SQS:OPT_IN_REQUIRED
- SQS:OVER_LIMIT
- SQS:PARAMETER_OUT_OF_RANGE
- SQS:REQUEST_EXPIRED
- SQS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SQS:S3_BUCKET_ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:S3_BUCKET_NOT_FOUND
- SQS:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SQS:THROTTLING_EXCEPTION

### Remove Permission

`<sqs:remove-permission>`

Removes a permission from this message queue.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Label</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name for the permission to remove.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the queue in which permissions are deleted from.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SQS:ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:CONNECTIVITY
- SQS:INTERNAL_FAILURE
- SQS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SQS:INVALID_DATA
- SQS:OPT_IN_REQUIRED
- SQS:REQUEST_EXPIRED
- SQS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SQS:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SQS:THROTTLING_EXCEPTION

### Send Message

`<sqs:send-message>`

Sends a message to a specified queue. The message must be between 1 and 256K bytes long.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Message">Message</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Message to send.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the queue in which to send the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#SendMessageResult">Send Message Result</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SQS:ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:CONNECTIVITY
- SQS:INTERNAL_FAILURE
- SQS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SQS:INVALID_DATA
- SQS:MESSAGE_SIZE_THRESHOLD_OUT_OF_RANGE
- SQS:OPT_IN_REQUIRED
- SQS:PARAMETER_OUT_OF_RANGE
- SQS:REQUEST_EXPIRED
- SQS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SQS:S3_BUCKET_ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:S3_BUCKET_NOT_FOUND
- SQS:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SQS:THROTTLING_EXCEPTION

### Send Message Batch

`<sqs:send-message-batch>`

Delivers up to 10 messages to the specified queue. This is a batch version of **Send Message**.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Messages</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#Message">Message</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of Message items.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the queue in which to send the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#BatchResult">Batch Result</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SQS:ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:CONNECTIVITY
- SQS:INTERNAL_FAILURE
- SQS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SQS:INVALID_DATA
- SQS:MESSAGE_SIZE_THRESHOLD_OUT_OF_RANGE
- SQS:OPT_IN_REQUIRED
- SQS:PARAMETER_OUT_OF_RANGE
- SQS:REQUEST_EXPIRED
- SQS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SQS:S3_BUCKET_ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:S3_BUCKET_NOT_FOUND
- SQS:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SQS:THROTTLING_EXCEPTION

### Set Queue Attributes

`<sqs:set-queue-attributes>`

Sets the value of one or more queue attributes, which can take up to 60 seconds to propagate
throughout the SQS system (although changes made to the MessageRetentionPeriod attribute can take up
to 15 minutes).

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map of attributes to set.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Queue Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the queue.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RequestIDAttribute">Request ID Attribute</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SQS:ACCESS_DENIED
- SQS:CONNECTIVITY
- SQS:INTERNAL_FAILURE
- SQS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- SQS:INVALID_DATA
- SQS:OPT_IN_REQUIRED
- SQS:REQUEST_EXPIRED
- SQS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SQS:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SQS:THROTTLING_EXCEPTION

## Object Types

- [Batch Result](#BatchResult)
- [Batch Result Error Entry](#BatchResultErrorEntry)
- [Change Message Visibility Batch Request Entry](#ChangeMessageVisibilityBatchRequestEntry)
- [CRL File](#CrlFile)
- [Custom OCSP Responder](#CustomOcspResponder)
- [Delete Message Batch Request Entry](#DeleteMessageBatchRequestEntry)
- [Expiration Policy](#ExpirationPolicy)
- [Keystore](#KeyStore)
- [Large Payload Support](#LargePayloadSupport)
- [Message](#Message)
- [Reconnect](#Reconnect)
- [Reconnect Forever](#ReconnectForever)
- [Reconnection](#Reconnection)
- [Redelivery Policy](#RedeliveryPolicy)
- [Request ID Attribute](#RequestIDAttribute)
- [Send Message Result](#SendMessageResult)
- [Standard Revocation Check](#StandardRevocationCheck)
- [TLS](#Tls)
- [Truststore](#TrustStore)

### Batch Result

For each message in the batch, the response contains a String representing the entry ID if the
message succeeds or a BatchResultErrorEntry tag if the message fails.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Failed</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#BatchResultErrorEntry">BatchResultErrorEntry</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of <a href="#BatchResultErrorEntry">Batch Result Error Entry</a> items.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Successful</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of String items representing the entry ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Batch Result Error Entry

Used in the responses of a batch API to give a detailed description of the result of an action on
each entry in the request.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Code</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Error code that represents why the action failed on this entry.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of an entry in a batch request.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Message that explains why the action failed on this entry.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sender Fault</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether the error happened due to the senderâs fault.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Change Message Visibility Batch Request Entry

Used to change the visibility timeout of messages.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Identifier for this particular receipt handle. This is used to communicate the result. The IDs of a batch request must be unique within the request.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Receipt Handle</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Receipt handle for this entry.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Visibility Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>New value (in seconds) for the messageâs visibility timeout.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### CRL File

Specifies the location of the certification revocation list (CRL) file.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the CRL file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom OCSP Responder

Configures a custom OCSP responder for certification revocation checks.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the OCSP responder.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cert Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response. If specified, the alias must be in the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Delete Message Batch Request Entry

Used to delete messages in batch.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Identifier for this particular receipt handle. This is used to communicate the result. The IDs of a batch request must be unique within the request.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Receipt Handle</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Receipt handle for this entry.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

Configures an expiration policy strategy.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the maximum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Max Idle Time</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Keystore

Configures the keystore for the TLS protocol. The keystore you generate contains a private key and a
public certificate.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the keystore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the key to use when the keystore contains multiple private keys. By default, Mule uses the first key in the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the keystore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Large Payload Support

Supports large message payloads on Amazon SQS.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Bucket</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the AWS S3 bucket in which to store large payload messages. The AWS S3 bucket must already be created and configured in AWS S3. Enabling this feature incurs additional charges for using AWS S3.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Size Threshold</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Message size threshold value for storing message payloads in the AWS S3 bucket. The default value for the message size threshold is 256 KB and the maximum threshold size value is 256 KB. The maximum message size is 2 GB.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>256</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Size Threshold Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Data unit for the <strong>Message Size Threshold</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>KB</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom S3 Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets a custom S3 endpoint. Useful when a non-standard service endpoint is required, such as a VPC endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>S3 Key Prefix</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Prefix to prepend to Amazon S3 object keys when storing large message payloads in the S3 bucket.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Message

Output attributes for a sent message.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>body</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Message body to send.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>deduplicationId</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Token for deduplicating sent messages. If multiple messages are sent with the same deduplication ID, Amazon SQS accepts the messages successfully, but delivers only the first message during the deduplication interval (5 minutes by default).</p></div><div><p>For FIFO queues, set <strong>ContentBasedDeduplication</strong> to <code>true</code> at a queue level to automatically generate the value for the <strong>deduplicationId</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>delaySeconds</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of seconds that a specific message is delayed. Valid values are <code>0</code> through <code>900</code>. If no value is specified, the default value for the queue is used.</p></div><div><p>The <strong>delaySeconds</strong> field cannot be set per message when <strong>FifoQueue</strong> is set; <strong>delaySeconds</strong> can only be set at a queue level.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>groupId</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Group this message belongs to in the FIFO queue. This parameter applies only to FIFO queues. When creating a FIFO queue, this parameter is required.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unique identifier for the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>message Attributes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map of typed key-value pairs to send as message attributes. You must specify a value, key, and data type for each entry.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>receipt Handle</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Receipt handle for this entry.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

Configures a standard reconnection strategy, which specifies how often to reconnect and how many
reconnection attempts the connector source or operation can make.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts the Mule app can make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector source or operation attempts to
reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

Configures a reconnection strategy for an operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if, when an app is deployed, a connectivity test does not pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Allow the deployment to fail.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Ignore the results of the connectivity test.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

Configures the redelivery policy for executing requests that generate errors. You can add a
redelivery policy to any source in a flow.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Redelivery Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of times that a redelivered request can be processed unsuccessfully before returning a REDELIVERY_EXHAUSTED error.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Use Secure Hash</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Digest Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Secure hashing algorithm to use if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>true</code>. If the payload of the message is a Java object, Mule ignores this value and returns the value that the payloadâs <code>hashCode()</code> returned.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more expressions that determine when a message was redelivered. This property can be set only if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>false</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object Store</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the object store that stores the redelivery counter for each message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Request ID Attribute

Used for a request ID.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Request Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AWS request ID used when a service call isnât working as expected, and you need to work with AWS support to debug an issue.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Send Message Result

Sends the result of the message.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>MD5 Of Message Attributes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MD5 digest of the non-URL-encoded message attribute string. This can be used to verify that Amazon SQS received the message correctly. The first URL in Amazon SQS decodes the message before creating the MD5 digest. For information about MD5, refer to <a href="http://www.faqs.org/rfcs/rfc1321.html">RFC 1321 - The MD5 Message-Digest Algorithm</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>MD5 Of Message Body</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MD5 digest of the non-URL-encoded message body string. This can be used to verify that Amazon SQS received the message correctly. The first URL in Amazon SQS decodes the message before creating the MD5 digest. For information about MD5, refer to <a href="http://www.faqs.org/rfcs/rfc1321.html">RFC 1321 - The MD5 Message-Digest Algorithm</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Element containing the message ID of the message sent to the queue. For more information, refer to <a href="https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/sqs-queue-message-identifiers.html">Amazon SQS Queue and Message Identifiers</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

Configures standard revocation checks for TLS certificates.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Which elements to verify in the certificate chain:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Verify only the last element in the certificate chain.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Verify all elements in the certificate chain.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Check the Certification Revocation List (CRL) for certificate validity.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Use the Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP) to check certificate validity.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use the secondary method to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Use the method that wasnât specified in the <strong>Prefer Crls</strong> field (the secondary method) to check certificate validity.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Do not use the secondary method to check certificate validity.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if the revocation server canât be reached or is busy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Avoid verification failure.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Allow the verification to fail.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### TLS

Configures TLS to provide secure communications for the Mule app.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Truststore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Keystore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#StandardRevocationCheck">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CustomOcspResponder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CrlFile">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a revocation checking mechanism.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Truststore

Configures the truststore for TLS.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the truststore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the truststore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule stops performing certificate validations. Setting this to <code>true</code> can make connections vulnerable to attacks.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Amazon SQS Connector 5.12 Studio Configuration

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure a source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-input-source).
4.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).

When you run the connector, you can view the app log to check for problems, as described in
[View the App Log](#view-app-log).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, see the
[Amazon SQS Connector Reference](amazon-sqs-connector-reference#node-behavior).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add Amazon SQS Connector to your Mule project to automatically populate the XML code with the
connectorâs namespace and schema location and add the required dependencies to the projectâs
`pom.xml` file:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In **Add Modules to Project**, type `amazon sqs` in the search field.
3.  Click **Amazon SQS** in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure a Source

A source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. You can configure one of these input
sources to use with Amazon SQS Connector:

- **Receive messages**  
  Retrieves one or more messages from the specified queue
- **HTTP > Listener**  
  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port
- **Scheduler**  
  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure the **Receive messages** source operation, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Amazon SQS > Receive messages**.
2.  Drag **Receive messages** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **Receive messages** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the **Display
    Name** field.
4.  Specify values for the following fields:
    - **Visibility Timeout**  
      Period of time during which other consumers are prevented from receiving and processing the
      same message in the queue
    - **Visibility Timeout unit**  
      The time unit for the Visibility Timeout:
      - NANOSECONDS
      - MICROSECONDS
      - MILLISECONDS
      - SECONDS
      - MINUTES
      - HOURS
      - DAYS

    - **Preserve Messages**  
      Preserve messages after they are read rather than immediately deleting them from the queue
      after they are read
    - **Number Of Messages**  
      Number of messages to retrieve
    - **Queue Url**  
      URL of the queue in which to receive messages

5.  If you are using the **Receive messages** source in cluster mode and want to receive messages on
    the primary node only, select the **Primary node only** checkbox on the **Receive messages >
    Advanced** tab:

    ![Read message configuration Advanced tab with Primary node only option selected](_images/amazon-sqs-primary-node-only.png)

    For more information, see
    [Receiving Messages in Cluster Mode](amazon-sqs-connector-config-topics#node-behavior).

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, itâs best to configure a global element that all instances of that
connector in the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to provide the
authentication credentials the connector uses to access the target Amazon SQS system. Amazon SQS
supports the Basic and Role authentication methods for connections.

You can either enter your credentials into the global configuration properties or reference a
configuration placed in a file in the `src` folder of your project file that contains these values.
For simpler maintenance and better reuse of your project, use a configuration file.

1.  Drag a connector operation onto the Studio canvas.
2.  To create a global element for the connector, click the **Global Elements** tab at the base of
    the canvas, and then click **Create**.
3.  In the **Choose Global Type** window, expand **Connector Configuration** and select **Amazon SQS
    Configuration**:

    ![Select Connector for Global Element](_images/amazon-sqs-studio-select-global.png)

4.  Click **OK**.
5.  Specify the global element properties:

    ![Configure Global Element](_images/amazon-sqs-studio-global-config-new.png)

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>Name used to reference the configuration</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Session Token</p></td><td><p>Session token used to validate the temporary security credentials</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access Key</p></td><td><p>Alphanumeric text string that uniquely identifies the user who owns the account</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Secret Key</p></td><td><p>Key that acts as a password</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Try AWS Credentials Provider Chain</p></td><td><p>Drop-down menu that controls whether to use temporary credentials</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Region Endpoint</p></td><td><p>Regional endpoint to process your requests</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SQS Endpoint</p></td><td><p>SQS endpoint for Amazon VPC support.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Default Global URL</p></td><td><p>URL of the Amazon SQS queue to act upon</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    When you provide a queue URL in the **Default Global Queue URL** field of the global element,
    the connector automatically creates the queue and sets the URL of this queue as the provided
    value. All Amazon SQS message processors that reference the global element perform operations
    using this queue URL.

    To reference a different queue URL for a particular message processor in the flow, perform the
    operation using the `Queue URL` attribute provided by the message processor.

6.  Leave the default entries for the **Proxy** tab.
7.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that the parameters of your global configuration are
    accurate and that Mule is able to connect successfully to your instance of Amazon SQS.  
    To test the connection, you must specify a value for the **Test Queue Arn** field in the
    configuration. If a value is not specified, the test connection is not performed, and the
    console displays a warning when the operation is invoked.
8.  Click **OK**.

## View the App Log

To check for problems, you can view the app log as follows:

- If youâre running the app from Anypoint Platform, the output is visible in the Anypoint Studio
  console window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log is visible in your OS
  console.

Unless the log file path is customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view the
app log in the default location `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`.

## Next Step

After configuring this connector in Studio, see
[Additional Configuration Information](amazon-sqs-connector-config-topics) for more configuration
steps.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Troubleshooting Amazon SQS Connector 5.12

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

To troubleshoot Anypoint Connector for Amazon SQS (Amazon SQS Connector), become familiar with the
information about interpreting commonly thrown exception messages.

## Enable SSL Logging

Enable SSL logs to investigate an issue related to TLS communications.

Set your SSL log level depending on your required level of detail:

1.  Enable only SSL log details:

    `javax.net.debug=ssl`

2.  Enable only handshaking details. (The handshake protocol is a series of messages exchanged over
    the record protocol.)

    `javax.net.debug=ssl:handshake`

3.  Enable handshaking details and SSL details at the same time:

    `javax.net.debug=ssl, handshake`

4.  Enable the dumping of all details and traffic data:

    `javax.net.debug=all`

    > [!NOTE] This option is very verbose and under normal circumstances is not necessary.

> [!WARNING] SSL logging results in a performance impact for HTTPS or other TLS connections. Enable
> SSL logging only to troubleshoot a specific issue and do not enable it for extended periods. SSL
> logging produces a significant amount of log messages and can inundate your log file if it is left
> enabled and unattended.

To enable SSL logs:

Set the debug parameter as an argument in the runtime configuration for the application.

1.  In Studio, right-click on the project and select **Run > Run Configurations**.
2.  Go to the **Arguments** tab and add `-M-Djavax.net.debug=ssl` in the **VM arguments** section.

## Enable Verbose Exception Logging

Enable verbose exception logs to show a complete stack trace of the error instead of the default
truncated output:

1.  In Studio, right-click on the project and select **Run > Run Configurations**.
2.  Go to the **Arguments** tab and append the arguments in the **VM arguments** section to add the
    `mule.verbose.exceptions` property. For example:

    `-XX:PermSize=128M -XX:MaxPermSize=256M -Dmule.verbose.exceptions=true`

## Enable Verbose Logging

To get a better understanding of why an applicationâs interaction with Amazon SQS Connector is
failing, temporarily enable verbose logging for the connector.

Remember to always disable enhanced verbosity after troubleshooting, because it can affect your Mule
applicationâs performance.

To enable verbose logging in the configuration file:

1.  Access Anypoint Studio and navigate to the **Package Explorer** view.
2.  Open your applicationâs project name.
3.  Open the `src/main/resources` folder.
4.  Open the `log4j2.xml` file inside the folder.
5.  Add an `<AsyncLogger>` tag inside the `<Loggers>` tag:

    ```xml
    			<Loggers>
    				...
    				<AsyncLogger name="org.mule.extension.sqs" level="DEBUG"/>
    				...
    			</Loggers>
    ```

6.  Save your application changes.
7.  Click the project name in **Package Explorer** and then click **Run** > **Run As** > **Mule
    Application**.

## Troubleshoot AWS STS Regionalized Endpoints

In AWS SDK v2, the endpoint that the STS client of the `AssumeRoleConnectionProvider` uses to get
session tokens is now regionalized instead of global. See the
[AWS STS Regionalized endpoints](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdkref/latest/guide/feature-sts-regionalized-endpoints.html)
documentation for details. As explained in the
[AWS Security Token Service endpoints and quotas](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/sts.html)
documentation, regionalized endpoints shouldnât cause changes in the connectorâs functionality. AWS
recommends using Regional STS endpoints to reduce latency, build in redundancy, and increase session
token validation.

If you encounter any issues as a result of the change in endpoints to regionalized, you can force
Amazon SQS Connector to use the global endpoint by specifying the URL endpoint
`https://sts.amazonaws.com` in the **Custom STS Endpoint** field located in the **Advanced** tab of
the **Global Elements** properties configuration window of the connector.

## Understand Commonly Thrown Exceptions

Here is a list of commonly thrown exception messages and how to interpret them:

- SQS:ACCESS_DENIED

  The user does not have permission to execute this operation.

- SQS:CONNECTIVITY

  The connection to the Amazon SQS server is interrupted.

- SQS:INTERNAL_FAILURE

  The request fails due to an unknown error, exception, or failure.

- SQS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS

  Authentication against the Amazon SQS server fails due to invalid credentials.

- SQS:INVALID_DATA

  The test connection cannot be performed because the Test Queue ARN parameter is not set.

- SQS:MESSAGE_NOT_IN_FLIGHT

  The specified message is not in flight.

- SQS:MESSAGE_SIZE_THRESHOLD_OUT_OF_RANGE

  The message threshold size is not between 0 and 256 KB.

- SQS:OPT_IN_REQUIRED

  The AWS access key ID requires a subscription for the service.

- SQS:OVER_LIMIT

  The attempted action exceeds a limit, for example, if the Recieve Messages source receives the
  maximum number of inflight messages or if the Add Permission operation reaches the maximum number
  of permissions for the queue.

- SQS:PARAMETER_OUT_RANGE

  The value is not within the specified range.

- SQS:PURGE_QUEUE_IN_PROGRESS

  The specified queue receives a Purge Queue request within the last 60 seconds.

- SQS:QUEUE_ALREADY_EXISTS

  There is a queue with this name already and the request includes different attributes from the
  existing queue with this name.

- SQS:QUEUE_DELETED_RECENTLY

  A queue with the same name is deleted within the last 60 seconds.

- SQS:REQUEST_EXPIRED

  The request reaches the service more than 15 minutes after the request date stamp or the request
  expiration date, or the request date stamp is more than 15 minutes in the future.

- SQS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

  The retries of a certain execution block are exhausted.

- SQS:S3_BUCKET_ACCESS_DENIED

  The server returns an AmazonSQSException with a 403 HTTP status code.

- SQS:S3_BUCKET_NOT_FOUND

  The server returns an AmazonSQSException with a 404 HTTP status code.

- SQS:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE

  The server is experiencing a temporary failure.

- SQS:THROTTLING_EXCEPTION

  The request fails due to request throttling.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Amazon SQS Connector Reference](amazon-sqs-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [Known Issues](https://help.salesforce.com/s/issues)
---

# Upgrading and Migrating Amazon SQS Connector to 5.12

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Upgrade Anypoint Connector for Amazon SQS to 5.12 to improve and optimize the performance of your
projects using long polling, multithreading, and more control over the polling parameters.

## Supported Upgrade Paths

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>From Version</th><th>To Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>5.x</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5.12</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### 5.12

- Amazon SQS Connector now supports passing an external ID in Role-based authentication.
- In the **Receive messages** source, the **Time unit** field is renamed to **Start delay time
  unit**, and its default value is changed from `MILLISECONDS` to `SECONDS`.

  > [!NOTE] This was changed back in v5.12.2. The field in the **Receive messages** source is now
  > called **Time unit** again, and its default value is set back to `MILLISECONDS`.

### 5.11

All of the Amazon SQS Connector error types are now exported. You can see specific error messages
instead of MULE:UNKNOWN.

### 5.10

Amazon SQS Connector now supports the addition of an SQS endpoint, which you can use with Amazon
Virtual Private Cloud (VPC).

### 5.9

With release 5.9 you now have the ability to enable long polling for the Read Message source and
Read operation and to control the time interval for the polling. Long polling can reduce the number
of network requests without affecting throughput.

Additionally, you can now configure the number of threads for your sources, allowing for a higher
throughput in some situations.

### 5.8

Amazon SQS Connector now supports sending and receiving large messages with payloads of up to 2 GB.
This is beneficial when your application requires large amounts of data to be stored in queues.

> [!NOTE] This feature requires the use of the Amazon S3 service. Large payload transmissions are
> billed as one SQS request and one S3 request. Billing for the payload is based on the amount of
> data stored in Amazon S3.

### 5.7

The Messages object now contains a new `deduplicationId` field. If multiple messages are sent with
the same deduplication ID, Amazon SQS accepts the messages successfully, but delivers only the first
message during the 5-minute deduplication interval.

### 5.6

Cluster node support was added back to the `Receive Messages` input source and works the same as it
did in version 5.4.3. The input source listens for messages on all cluster nodes by default, but you
can change this behavior by using the `Primary node only` field on the `Advanced` sub-tab on the
`Receive Messages` tab. For more information, see
[Receiving Messages in Cluster Mode](amazon-sqs-connector-config-topics#node-behavior).

### 5.5

Version 5.5.0 was deprecated because cluster mode support was removed from that version.

### 5.4.2

The Amazon SDK for Java dependency was upgraded to version 1.11.649.

## Requirements

Ensure your system meets the following requirements before you perform the upgrade:
